title: DayStarter Labs
preview:
Inbound PLG signup. Solo founder (iOS dev) experimenting with chat + semantic search for a tiny customer service automation in-app. Created account 2026-03-01, used free credits to run prompt tests. Observed good latency on small prompts; ran into rate limit errors during parallel test harness on 2026-03-13. No AE touch yet — flagged as inbound-growth. Wants hosted API only (public cloud).

Shorthand from signup form: "early prototype, budget tiny, need quick integration into iOS app, prefer predictable per-token pricing, no enterprise SSO now".

Key quotes from in-app feedback form: "I need embeddings + chat that just work — low ops, low cost. If the demo shows <100ms p95 for short prompts I'm sold."

Asks: sample iOS SDK snippet, client-side key rotation guidance, basic cost calc for 100k tokens/mo. Interested in benching Redwood-hosted model vs third-party hosted endpoints (cost/latency).

title: BryteBridge Marketing Platform
preview:
Discovery notes (concise):
- Inbound from content marketing campaign (signup form + demo request).
- Company: BryteBridge — SMB marketing platform that auto-generates campaign content (multi-tenant SaaS for small brands).
- Primary ask: high-volume, low-cost text generation for daily newsletter + social + landing page variants.
- Volume: estimated 8M tokens/month in steady state; initial POC target = 1.2M tokens/month.
- Latency/throughput: p50 target <250ms for single prompt flows used by editor UI; bulk batch jobs run asynchronously (throughput 200-400 reqs/sec during batch windows).
- Cost sensitivity: high. Current vendor spend ~ $2.5k/month; want better unit economics at scale.
- Technical preference: open-models/quantized variants acceptable; interested in Redwood's model catalog + quantization guidance.
- Critical features called out: prefix/KV caching for template-heavy content, automatic batching for burst windows, deterministic routing to minimize cost, and simple REST/SDK integration for self-serve.
- Security: SOC2 acceptable, want audit logs and basic retention controls. No air-gapped needs; public cloud only.
- Quote from call: "We need predictable per-item pricing — otherwise ops margin vanishes at scale."
- Product fit: Hosted API looks aligned with self-serve motion. Willing to start with credits/POC then convert if unit economics look right.
- POC plan: 2-week gated POC — deliver sample run using 100k tokens across representative templates, measure latency, token cost, and cache hit-rate.
- Integration notes: prefer node.js SDK; will plug into existing content pipeline that uses template placeholders and per-customer tuning.
- Observability ask: per-route token/cost breakdown, simple tracing for slow requests.
- Internal stakeholders: CTO (product owner), Growth lead (campaign owner), 1 backend eng for POC.
- Sales notes: AE (Ava Chen) intro call 2026-02-25; demo scheduled 2026-03-02 (recording + Fireflies). SE Liam joined for deep tech questions on 2026-02-27.
- Competitive posture: evaluating OpenAI and Cohere pricing + caching approaches.
- Risk: may stick with incumbent if Redwood POC doesn't show >=20% cost reduction or similar latency.
- Next steps (short): send tailored pricing sketch + example POC plan (by 2026-03-15), run 2-week POC, validate cache-hit improvements.

title: BridgeFlux Support Systems
preview:
Lead source: inbound via product signup (self-serve). Company builds "FluxAgent" — an agent layer for small-mid market support teams. Wants to integrate Redwood hosted API during a 10-day POC.

Key requirements (verbatim & shorthand captured):
- Workloads: high volume short-text ticket summarization + structured JSON outputs for CRM ingestion. Also direct tool-calls to update ticket status in their system (webhook-based).
- Latency/throughput: aiming for ~150-250ms p95 for single-turn summary calls; expected concurrency 5-15 qps during peak; bursty behavior from shared inbox.
- Cost sensitivity: SMB — "cost per agent" matters; wants numbers for 7B quantized vs 13B hosted variants. Asked: "Can we run cheap open models for summaries and fallback to higher-quality for escalations?"
- Model preference: open-model friendly; prefers hosted open models in Redwood catalog (7B quantized for majority, 13B for fallback). Willing to try Redwood Optimize suggestions for batching.
- Structured output: strict JSON schema required for their CRM (fields: summary_short, summary_long, top_tags[], action_items[]). Needs example schema and Redwood's structured output reliability guidance.
- Tool calling: must be able to invoke a ticket-update webhook with authentication; needs SDK examples for node/python. Wants sample function-call patterns for idempotent updates and rollback on failure.
- Observability: wants per-route latency and per-token cost breakdown in Console. Need retention of logs for 30 days.
- Security: requires SSO/SAML for team, SOC2 attestation, audit logs; data residency not strict (US preferred).

POC scope agreed: 10 days hosted API POC — endpoints: /summarize (streaming), /structured (JSON), /tool-call (secure webhook). Goals: demonstrate <250ms p95 for summaries, structured output accuracy >90 on curated prompt set, and safe tool-call retries.

Conversation snippets:
- "We love the quick sign-up — want to see how it fits into our FluxAgent flow before committing." — VP Product
- "If we can get reliable JSON output and cheap per-agent costs we'll expand to 150 agents after POC." — Head of Ops

Stubs/attachments: pricing deck path (drive), security Q uploaded (SUPPORT-1123). Gmail thread contains initial self-serve signup logs. Fireflies transcript ff-20260215 includes call timestamps and action items.

Internal notes: good PLG motion — engaged engineering team, receptive to Optimize recommendations. Primary risk: cost sensitivity and SOC2 ask (we should surface SOC2 letter + quickstart for hosted API). SE to prepare example toolbox with batching/quantization tradeoffs and sample Node/Python snippets for function-calling webhook flow.

Next steps (action items):
- Maya: send sandbox API creds + quickstart + tailored pricing sketch (today).
- Ethan: prepare POC checklist and fidelity targets; include sample JSON schema tests and streaming example (due 2026-03-15).
- Follow-up demo: schedule 30m POC walkthrough week of 2026-03-16.

title: Brighton Helix Chat
preview:
Inbound SMB demo lead from Product Hunt link — lightweight B2B chat SDK maker. Early-stage (pre-series A), 60 engineers+product. Want to embed a streaming assistant into their product (in-app chat + contextual suggestions). Key asks: low token-latency (p50 ~100ms), streaming partial responses to keep UX snappy, configurable fallback routing when quota hits. Prefer open/compatible model flavors (quantized Llama variants or other open LLMs) but will evaluate Redwood hosted first for speed. Cost sensitive — expects ~5k-20k ARR initially from hosted API usage; wants clear per-token breakdown.

Sales notes (shorthand):
- "We need to make the assistant feel instantaneous" — Priya (Dev Lead)
- Will demo in-app flow using small client app next week.
- Trial org already has API keys, devs started integration (websocket + streaming). Observed ~140-180ms p50 in initial tests (non-optimized). Wants to see kernel/runtime tuning + batching suggestions.
- Security: SOC2 required, SSO later; retention 30d default ok but may request audit logs for enterprise later.
- Routing/fallback: must be able to route to cheaper model variant on quota exhaustion w/ graceful degradation of responses.

POC ask: 48-hour performance run on hosted_api with 1k parallel short sessions, streaming enabled; capture p50/p90 latency and token cost per convo. SE to provide perf checklist and sample load script. EA to secure buy-in from VP Product.

title: GnomeForge Systems
preview:
Inbound PLG lead from indie dev + micro-startup. Founder/CTO (Elliot Park) building a tiny multi-tenant coding assistant for indie SaaS customers. Signed up via docs and quickstart; no AE outreach yet. Key points: prefers hosted API (fast time-to-market), will likely stick with public cloud. Current sandbox usage: experimenting with chat + embeddings for code search. Quote from call: "We need something that 'just works' — latency under ~250ms p50 for short chats, and simple token billing we can pass to customers." Budget: very cost-sensitive; target < $1k/mo initially, with willingness to ramp if product-market fit. Wants to test smaller open models first (Llama-family, distilled) but may use Redwood-verified models if cost/perf is right. Security: expects basic audit logs and 30d retention; not asking for VPC or private deployment now. Operational concerns raised: rate limits per sub-tenant, ability to rotate keys programmatically, and one-off high-burst days (deploy-time traffic). POC asks: sample prompts for code assistant, recommended model/quantization profile for low-cost inference, batching suggestions (automated vs manual).

CRM-style fragments/shorts observed in notes:
- "no AE yet; founder doing evals"
- "wants hosted only for now"
- "prefers US region/residency"
- "asks about token overflow protection"

Next small tactical actions (internal):
- Add to automated onboarding drip focusing on cost-preserving patterns (batching, cache), include short how-to for embedding-based code search.
- CS/growth to monitor usage; if >2k tokens/day create inbound AE alert.
- Provide quickstart snippet for rotating keys + sample prompt set for code assistant (attach to Drive doc).

title: Ceramic Orchard Helpdesk
preview:
Quick inbound trial signup (self-serve) — CRM lead from organic blog post on quick prompt guides.

Firmographics: SMB helpdesk SaaS, ~120 employees, primarily NA HQ, 30k monthly active support conversations. Looking to add conversational assistant and fast summarization for agent inbox.

Initial ask: "We signed up and love the simplicity. Production concern: responses feel slow for EU customers — we see 800-1,200 ms extra lag compared to US. Is there an easy region config?" — spoken by Head of Product on 2026-03-05 demo call.

What we've done so far (timeline):
- 2026-02-25: Self-serve signup, free credit used.
- 2026-03-02: Auto-instrumented latency metrics show p50 p95 differences by region; user reported EU p95 ~1.8s vs US p95 ~650ms.
- 2026-03-05: Discovery call (Fireflies ff_20260305_8923). Demo of hosted_api, basic SDK usage.
- 2026-03-08: Customer tried switching region param in SDK to eu-west-1; saw minor improvement but still high tail latency. Provided quick checklist (use short prompts, enable streaming, reduce max_tokens).
- 2026-03-10: Raised INF-5123 for deeper investigation; engineering performed a synthetic run and flagged prompt length and batching misconfiguration.

Technical profile & requirements: 
- Primary workload: short chat-turns for agent assist (single-turn + context), and ticket summarization (concise outputs).
- Latency targets: p95 < 900ms for chat, p95 < 2s for summarization in EU.
- Throughput: bursty — 7–20 rps during business hours per tenant. Cost sensitivity: moderate — prefers hosted usage but wants configuration to reduce token costs.
- Model preference: open/verified mid-size models acceptable if latency/cost tradeoff documented.
- Routing/fallback: prefer region-aware routing to nearest data center; fallback to US region only if explicitly allowed.

Observed issues and hypotheses: 
- Calls from EU routed to US by default in their SDK usage; region param not persisted in client.
- Customers sending long system prompts + full conversation history on every request (no prefix caching).
- SDK default timeout is low for some EU network conditions; some client-side retries amplify tail latency.

Immediate recommendations provided to customer: 
1) Verify region parameter is set at client init and not per-request. Example snippet shared in call notes (drive:1Perf-Playbook).
2) Shorten system prompts and move stable context to prefix cache in app layer.
3) Enable streaming to reduce TTFB for first tokens; reduce max_tokens for summarization job (chunk long tickets).
4) Increase client-side read timeout to 10s and add jittered retry policy (1 retry) to avoid thundering retries.
5) Consider model profile with lower latency (quantized mid-size) — SE to share recommended list and perf numbers.

Customer quotes and tone: 
- "We need sub-second perceived response for agents — anything slower kills adoption." — Head of Product.
- Tone: pragmatic, open to tuning, but needs quick wins before moving to paid tier.

Next steps (owned):
- SE (Maya) to run an EU synthetic test and produce p50/p95 matrix for three model profiles (due 2026-03-16).
- Schedule 60m perf tuning workshop w/ eng + product (propose 2026-03-18).
- Send short SDK snippet and region-check checklist (done, linked).

Potential blockers: finance signoff, team bandwidth to implement caching, and verifying that EU data residency/latency acceptable under hosted offering.

CRM shorthand: discovery -> high intent but needs perf fixes. Keep close, two-week cadence for follow-ups.

title: EmberFox Payments
preview:
Inbound from product-led signup. Sees immediate value in embeddings for semantic search and chat assistant for merchant support.

Primary contact: CTO - Maya Patel (also handles infra/security). Quote: 'We need SOC2 evidence before procurement but can trial hosted API for 30 days.'

Latency/throughput: target p95 < 300ms for short chat turn, expect ~40-60 concurrent agents during business hours.

Model preference: cost-conscious; prefer quantized open models or small LLMs for initial deployment. Will test 2-3 variants.

Routing/fallback: interested in automatic fallback to cheaper model when load spikes; want easy pinning per API key.

Data handling: transactional data may include last4 PAN and customer notes => ask for redaction guidance and retention controls.

Security asks: SSO (SAML) integration, SOC2 report (type II) timeline, audit logs, KMS integration for key control. Data residency: US only for now.

POC plan: 2-week hosted POC using sample merchant transcripts + synthetic transaction data. Success metrics: intent accuracy > 85% for support intents, embedding recall@10 > 0.8 for merchant docs.

Billing: budget signalled $10k-$50k ARR if POC successful. Wants breakout of per-token cost vs embedding indexing cost.

Competitive notes: trialling OpenAI for baseline; Redwood needs to show lower cost for bulk embeddings and transparent performance numbers.

Action items: send SSO integration guide, share SOC2 redacted artefact (or SOC2 scope timeline), provide VPC cost estimation, provision POC API key and sample prompt templates.

title: BerryTrail AI
preview:
Lead source: inbound (community meetup & blog).
Summary: small ecommerce platform (~120 employees) building conversational product discovery + support assistant. Goal is to replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranker, then add a chat layer for product guidance. Very cost conscious but prioritizes non-hallucinating answers for SKU/spec info.
What we heard on 3/9 call:
- "Customer quote: 'We need reliable answers about SKUs and specs — hallucinations cost us returns.'"
- Current infra: frontend + search on AWS; wants hosted_api (public cloud) to prototype quickly, then evaluate Dedicated later if traffic grows.
- POC scope: embeddings for 1M product text tokens (catalog + specs + reviews), reranker for top-10, chat for post-search follow-up. Expected POC volume: ~50k queries/month.
- Latency targets: embeddings batch job OK to be minutes; chat interactions need p95 <= 250ms ideally; realistic goal 150-250ms for 7B variants. Throughput: spikes to ~30 req/s during promos.
- Model selection concerns: team asked about Llama-2-13B vs Mistral-7B-instruct vs Qwen-7B-chat. title: Cobalt Nimbus LLC
preview:
Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signup on hosted API. Primary contact: Maria Lopez (CTO). Use case: conversational agent that calls internal tools (ticket search, run diagnostics) via function/tool-calls; need structured JSON output to map to internal RPC.

Summary of problem: In streaming + JSON_MODE the model sometimes emits function_call events in multiple chunks. The Node SDK handler receives first chunk containing 'function_call' and partial 'arguments' string; later chunks complete it. Customer's parser tries to JSON.parse on first visible chunk and fails with 'Unexpected token' errors. Also observed cases where model returns single quotes instead of double quotes inside arguments ("TypeError: Unexpected token '\' in JSON at position 0"). They attempted a server-side workaround to buffer until end_of_stream but that increases latency and complexity.

Customer also reports that when they enable strict function schema in their prompt, the model sometimes returns plain text instead of a function_call envelope, making schema validation fail with 'extraProperties' when they attempt to coerce into typed args.

What we've tried / suggested:
- Confirm Node SDK version (customer on 0.12.3) and suggested upgrade to latest; share streaming parser example.
- Reproducer repo requested (received 2026-03-10) with minimal server and client.
- Suggested approach: buffer per-request until 'DONE' event in stream or use stream event types to stitch function_call args before JSON.parse; SE will attempt to harden SDK example to make this easier.
- Discussed short-term workaround: disable JSON_MODE for function args and return as base64-encoded payload, then decode after full stream assembled (hacky but avoids parse errors).

Quotes / paraphrases: "We can accept a little overhead but need tool calls to start within 200ms of user action — can't wait for full buffer every time." — Maria L.

Open items: need raw chunk-level logs (we have partial), need exact SDK config and headers used, confirm if customer is using custom middleware that manipulates quotes. SE asked for HTTP-level capture (curl or tcpdump) to verify chunk boundaries.

Next steps: pairing session on 2026-03-14 to reproduce, capture raw stream, try patched SDK that defers JSON.parse until complete args stitched. If confirmed, provide code sample and suggest SDK patch/bug fix; if not solvable in hosted API SDK, propose short-term mitigation guidance for app-level buffering.

title: CodeRipple Labs
preview:
Lead: CodeRipple Labs — small devtools startup building an in-editor AI pair programmer (VS Code + web) focused on whole-file refactors and long context code search.

Stage: discovery — early inbound from founder (CTO ex-small-OSS biz). Self-serve sign-up completed, played with REST endpoint.

Primary asks / pain: 
- Long context windows: need 50k+ token window for whole-repo retrieval + code-aware summarization. Quote: "We need entire file + call graph preface for accurate suggestions."
- Rate limits: unpredictable bursts from IDE telemetry + background indexing; want clear per-key burst policy and ability to request higher short-term bursts.
- SDKs: wants first-class JS/TS SDK with streaming chunk events, reconnect and auth refresh hooks; also Python SDK for backend batch reranking.
- Latency: interactive suggestions target ~150-300ms token latency for short completions; tolerate higher for large refactors but want SLOs for small ops.
- Cost sensitivity: early-stage, careful about per-token spend; interested in batching/caching suggestions and prefix-KV caching.

Security / compliance: SMB buyer but asks for SSO/SAML for team, audit logs for key usage, and retention controls for uploaded corp repos. Not enterprise yet but will need SOC2 in 12-18mo roadmap.

POC plan: 2-week POC using hosted_api keys — integrate SDK in VS Code plugin + server-side reranker. Success criteria: maintain median latency <250ms for inline completions, cost <= $X/day (TBD), correct top-3 suggestions >= 70% on internal eval set.

Quotes & moments: 
- "If we can lock streaming + reconnect behavior we can move from PoC to paid within a month." — Lead engineer.
- Concern: "How do you handle sudden background indexing bursts? We can't have throttling that breaks UX."

Action items (owner in parens):
- Send concise hosted pricing comparison + example cost for 50k-context runs (Jordan).
- Share SDK roadmap and current JS streaming sample (Priya) — include auth-refresh example and telemetry hooks.
- Provide recommended rate-limit config for SMB/Devtools usage and quick note on burst application (Prod Ops).
- Schedule 30m tech call wk of 3/15 to walk architecture and POC plan (Jordan/Priya).

Artifacts: demo recording ff_20260309_001, quick-demo-deck in drive, initial infra notes attached (INF-324, INF-332).

title: Aurora Bridge Search
preview:
Shorthand notes from calls and threads:
- Company: Aurora Bridge Search, small legaltech startup building client-facing contract search + internal research.
- Primary ask: replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranking pipeline; want hosted API (self-serve) to move fast.
- Latency target: interactive search ~<300ms (p95) on 1-10 qps baseline; may scale to 30-50 qps during peak. Cost sensitive — want guidance on tokens vs embedding costs.
- Model preference: open to small/efficient text-embedding models; asked about support for OpenAI-style embeddings + local quantized alternatives.
- Vector DB question: currently evaluating Pinecone and Postgres (pgvector); asked whether Redwood hosted API has first-class connectors or recommended adapter patterns.
- Reranking: customer expects two-stage pipeline: ANN candidate retrieval -> rerank with cross-encoder/generation signal. Wants examples for combining Redwood generative scoring with embeddings.
- Data handling: public cloud ok, no air-gap. Asked about audit logs for queries (legal compliance) and SSO for admin console.
- Quote from CTO: "We need something that just works without ops overhead — self-serve to start, but must be production-ready if we grow."
- Action items: send short how-to for Pinecone + Redwood embeddings, provide cost estimate for 10k doc POC, sample reranking prompt templates, and link to hosted API quickstart.
- Risk: budget approval depends on Q2 hiring; security review expected to be light but must show audit logs and basic retention controls.
- Competitive signals: they trialed Pinecone + Hugging Face embeddings last month, hit cost/latency tradeoffs and manual rerank complexity. Open to switching if integration simpler.

title: DeckTrail Analytics
preview:
Inbound from sales enablement app maker. CTO (Maya Rivers) reached out via sales enablement Slack community — interested in Redwood hosted API for call summarization and search overlay.
- Prefers self-serve trial first; wants to validate accuracy and cost vs AssemblyAI + OpenAI.
- Main concerns: PII in transcripts (names, emails, credit-card like strings). Asked: 'Can we redact PII before storing? How long do transcripts live in your system?'
- Wants control over retention (30/90/never persist) and ability to disable logs for specific routes.
- Use-case: Chrome/Zoom connector collects transcript -> stream to Redwood for real-time summary + finalize summary stored in customer's DB. Also embeddings for search (vector store in customer infra later).
- Latency: soft requirement 300-800ms for streaming sentence-level summaries; cost-sensitive — target <$0.01 per processed minute.
- Security: small legal questionnaire sent; initial SOC2 mention ok but they asked specifically about audit logs, deletion APIs, and KMS for later stages.
- POC plan: ingest 200 meetings, compare summaries + redaction accuracy vs current vendor. AE to provide example retention policy and minimal demo config.
- Quote from call: 'We need to be able to guarantee we can strip PII before any long-term storage — that's non-negotiable for our early customers.'
- Next: send retention/PII FAQ + quickstart example with SDK redaction hooks; follow-up tech call with SE to demo streaming and retention settings.

title: Elm Harbor Assistants
preview:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve (free credits). Small engineering team (2 backend, 1 frontend). Primary product: chat-based support assistant + doc search across help center. Key pain: perceived slowness on user-facing chat — several customer complaints this week.

Observed behavior (customer reports / internal testing):
- Users in US experiencing ~600-900ms extra RTT vs EU users; account originally created with default region eu-west-1.
- Frontend shows request timeouts at ~12s (client-side) when prompts include long context + history (~1.5-3k tokens).
- Team tried increasing client timeout but worried about cost and UX.

What they've asked/said: 
- "We signed up fast, but users are saying the bot feels slow — is this something we can fix without moving infrastructure?" 
- Very cost-sensitive; prefer hosted API but may upgrade if clear perf wins.

Redwood quick wins suggested on call: 
- Region: suggest creating API key in us-west-2 region for US traffic; ran quick curl test (we provided sample) showed ~250ms median improvement in staging.
- Timeouts: recommend bumping client timeout to 30s temporarily for troubleshooting and using streaming to cut perceived latency for users.
- Prompt length: advise truncation of chat history to recent 3 turns + keep system prompt minimal; precompute embeddings for doc search to avoid sending huge context each request.
- Request-level settings: limit max_tokens for completions, reduce temperature for deterministic replies where applicable; enable chunked responses for long answers.
- Instrumentation: add simple timing on frontend to capture server vs network latency (we shared snippet).

Customer constraints/risks: 
- No enterprise contract; will not accept private deployment. Public cloud only.
- Needs SSO and basic audit logs (SOC2 planned but not required today).
- Low tolerance for trial cost creep.

Sales/next actions: 
- Send perf checklist + region test guide (drive doc).
- Schedule 30m troubleshooting with Marco + Ava to run region switch and sample prompt edits.
- If region swap + prompt changes cut p95 latency by >200ms, expect move to evaluation.

Quote from PO: "If we can make it snappier for US users without blowing our credits, we’ll commit to a paid plan."
